title: pilosella lactucella subsp. acutisquama
intro:
Pilosella lactucella subsp. acutisquama is a subspecies of flowering plant in the dandelion tribe within the sunflower family, Asteraceae, which is native to mountainous regions of Europe and Asia.

This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antipyretic properties, which can help alleviate various health issues.

The medicinal properties of Pilosella lactucella subsp. acutisquama are believed to provide several health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving pain, and treating skin conditions such as eczema and acne.

The plant can be used to prepare herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat various health conditions; however, more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and safety for human consumption.
actions: Pilosella lactucella subsp. acutisquama has many therapeutic actions, such as anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which help to reduce pain and inflammation in people with arthritis, improve wound healing in burn patients, and combat infections in individuals with compromised immune systems. The plant's antispasmodic and antihistamine effects also alleviate symptoms of allergies and asthma, making it easier for people to breathe and live comfortably in their daily lives. Additionally, its antiviral and antifungal properties help to prevent the spread of diseases, protecting people from illnesses such as the common cold and fungal infections. By incorporating the therapeutic actions of Pilosella lactucella subsp. acutisquama into their treatment plans, healthcare professionals can improve the quality of life for their patients, enabling them to engage in daily activities with greater ease and comfort.
constituents: Pilosella lactucella subsp. acutisquama has many active constituents, such as flavonoids, sesquiterpene lactones, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. These constituents have been found to exhibit anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities, and have been used to improve symptoms of conditions such as arthritis, digestive disorders, and skin infections. The flavonoids in Pilosella lactucella subsp. acutisquama, for example, have been shown to improve cardiovascular health by reducing blood pressure and cholesterol levels, while the sesquiterpene lactones have been found to have anti-cancer properties and improve symptoms of allergies and asthma. By utilizing these medicinal constituents, Pilosella lactucella subsp. acutisquama has the potential to improve people's daily lives by alleviating chronic pain, reducing the risk of chronic diseases, and promoting overall well-being.
